meaning: adj.不用稿子的,不用剧本的
examples: This included dramas, comedies, unscripted fare and newsmagazines.
Americans like debates because the candidates can be compared in an unscripted, live performance.
the popularity of unscripted programming has had the unexpected effect of ennobling its scripted counterpart.
The implications are clear for companies that would consider using Hangouts to field unscripted questions.
Volatility may pick up across the currency markets, particularly doing the unscripted question and answer session.
